article:
In brief: The heat and humidity are sticking around in Abuja for the next few days, with temperatures soaring into the mid-30s during the day and only dipping into the mid-20s at night. While we're not expecting much in the way of rain over the next week, things could start to get a bit more interesting towards the end of the forecast period. Let's dive into the details.

First off, let's talk about the current conditions. As of this morning, the temperature at the Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport was sitting at 24°C, which feels more like 30°C thanks to the high humidity. That's pretty typical for this time of year in Abuja, but it's still worth noting if you're planning on spending any time outdoors today. The humidity is a stifling 95%, which is making everything feel pretty muggy. The wind is almost non-existent, blowing at a mere 2 km/h, and the UV index is at a low 0, which means you don't have to worry too much about sunburn today.

Looking out at the city, the skies are a bit overcast, which is helping to keep the temperature from rising too quickly. [MAP: Satellite image of Abuja showing cloud cover] You can see from the satellite image that there's a decent amount of cloud cover over the city, which should help keep things relatively cool for the next few hours. However, as we head into the afternoon, those clouds should start to clear out, and the sun will start to beat down on us. If you're planning on heading out to the Jabi Lake or the National Arboretum, be sure to bring some sunscreen and a hat, even though the UV index is low.

### 2026-04-09
For tomorrow, we're expecting more of the same. The high temperature should rise to 34°C, with a low of 24°C overnight. There's a slight chance of rain, with a 28% chance of precipitation. That's not enough to make any significant changes to your plans, but it's worth keeping an eye on the skies just in case. [MAP: Forecast model showing precipitation chances] As you can see from the forecast model, the precipitation chances are pretty isolated, and it's unlikely that we'll see any widespread rain.

As we head into the evening, the temperature should start to drop off a bit, but it will still be pretty warm. If you're planning on heading out to dinner or a show, be sure to dress accordingly. The National Assembly complex and the surrounding areas are always bustling in the evenings, so be prepared for crowds and traffic.

### 2026-04-10
On Thursday, things are looking even hotter. The high temperature should rise to 37°C, with a low of 25°C overnight. There's almost no chance of rain, with a mere 3% chance of precipitation. [MAP: Temperature forecast showing heat index] As you can see from the temperature forecast, the heat index is going to be a major factor on Thursday, making it feel even hotter than it actually is. Be sure to stay hydrated and take breaks if you're going to be spending any time outdoors.

If you're planning on visiting the Abuja National Mosque or the Nigerian National Christian Centre, be sure to bring some water and a hat. The sun can be intense, especially in the late morning and early afternoon. And as always, be mindful of the traffic and congestion around these popular landmarks.

### 2026-04-11
On Friday, we're expecting more of the same heat, but with a slight drop in temperature. The high should be around 35°C, with a low of 24°C overnight. There's no chance of rain, so you can leave your umbrella at home. [MAP: Wind forecast showing calm conditions] As you can see from the wind forecast, it's going to be another calm day, with very little wind to speak of. That means the heat will feel even more oppressive, so be sure to take care of yourself.

As the weekend approaches, many of you will be heading out to the various markets and shopping centers around the city. Be sure to stay cool and hydrated, and don't hesitate to take a break if you need to. The Wuse Market and the Garki Market are always bustling on Fridays, so be prepared for crowds and congestion.

### 2026-04-12
On Saturday, the heat continues, with a high of 36°C and a low of 26°C overnight. There's still no chance of rain, so it's going to be a dry and sunny day. [MAP: Forecast model showing high pressure] As you can see from the forecast model, high pressure is dominating the region, which means we're not expecting any significant changes in the weather. That's good news if you have outdoor plans, but bad news if you were hoping for some relief from the heat.

If you're planning on heading out to the Millennium Park or the Hilton Abuja, be sure to bring some sunscreen and a hat. The sun can be intense, especially in the late morning and early afternoon. And as always, be mindful of the traffic and congestion around these popular landmarks.

### 2026-04-13
On Sunday, things are looking similar, with a high of 36°C and a low of 26°C overnight. There's a slight chance of rain, with an 8% chance of precipitation. [MAP: Precipitation forecast showing isolated showers] As you can see from the precipitation forecast, any rain we do see will be isolated and likely won't amount to much. But it's still worth keeping an eye on the skies, just in case.

As the week winds down, many of you will be heading back to work or school on Monday. Be sure to stay cool and hydrated, and don't hesitate to take a break if you need to. The traffic and congestion around the city can be intense, especially during rush hour.

### 2026-04-14 and 2026-04-15
As we head into the next week, things are looking a bit more interesting. On Monday, the high temperature should be around 35°C, with a low of 27°C overnight. There's a 23% chance of rain, which is the highest chance we've seen all week. [MAP: Forecast model showing low pressure] As you can see from the forecast model, low pressure is starting to move into the region, which could bring some much-needed rain to the area.

On Tuesday, the high temperature should be around 33°C, with a low of 26°C overnight. There's a 29% chance of rain, which is even higher than Monday. [MAP: Precipitation forecast showing scattered showers] As you can see from the precipitation forecast, we're expecting scattered showers to move into the area, which could bring some relief from the heat. We'll be keeping a close eye on the situation and will let you know if anything changes.

Overall, it's looking like a hot and dry week ahead, with plenty of sunshine and not much chance of rain. But as we head into the next week, things could start to get a bit more interesting. We'll be keeping a close eye on the forecast and will let you know if anything changes. Stay cool and hydrated, and we'll see you back here tomorrow.
